query: Why is my training crashing with CUDA out of memory?
log_snippet: RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 2.00 GiB (GPU 0; 10.76 GiB total capacity; 8.45 GiB already allocated)
gold_answer: The CUDA out of memory error typically occurs because your batch size is too large for your GPU memory. Try reducing batch_size, enabling gradient checkpointing with model.gradient_checkpointing_enable(), or using mixed precision training with torch.cuda.amp.autocast().

query: What is wrong with my batch normalization configuration?
log_snippet: RuntimeError: Expected more than 1 value per channel when training, got input size torch.Size([1, 64, 1, 1])
gold_answer: Batch normalization requires more than one sample per batch during training to compute meaningful statistics. When batch_size=1, the batch norm layer cannot compute variance. Use BatchNorm with batch_size >= 2, or switch to GroupNorm or LayerNorm which work with batch_size=1.

query: How do I fix the DataLoader worker error?
log_snippet: RuntimeError: DataLoader worker (pid 12345) is killed by signal: Bus error. It is possible that dataloader's workers are out of shared memory.
gold_answer: This error occurs when DataLoader workers run out of shared memory. Solutions: reduce num_workers, increase shared memory with --shm-size in Docker, set pin_memory=False, or reduce the batch size. You can also set num_workers=0 to disable multiprocessing.

query: Why is my loss NaN after a few iterations?
gold_answer: NaN loss is usually caused by: learning rate too high (gradients explode), numerical instability in loss computation, or bad data (zeros in log, negative values in sqrt). Try gradient clipping with torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(), reduce learning rate, or use mixed precision with loss scaling.

query: How do I enable automatic mixed precision in PyTorch?
gold_answer: Use torch.cuda.amp.autocast() and GradScaler. Wrap forward pass in autocast context: with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(): output = model(input). Then scale the loss: scaler.scale(loss).backward(), scaler.step(optimizer), scaler.update().
